sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Slack connector** integrates with Slack workspaces via the Slack Web API. It enables Shielva to sync messages, channels, and users in real-time.

This connector uses Bot Token authentication (xoxb-...), granting the bot access to the channels it is invited to — without requiring user-level OAuth flows.

**Key capabilities:**
- List all channels (public, private, DMs) in the workspace
- Sync message history from channels with cursor-based pagination
- List workspace members
- Fetch individual user profiles
- Incremental sync using the `oldest` timestamp parameter
- Stable document IDs via SHA-256(channel_id + ts) for deduplication

children.title: Connector Type
children.content: `slack` — registered in ACP as **Slack**.

children.title: Provider
children.content: Slack Technologies (Slack Web API — `https://slack.com/api/`)

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates bot_token presence; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE/MISSING_CREDENTIALS |
| `health_check()` | Calls `auth.test` to verify token; returns workspace name in message |
| `sync()` | Lists all public channels, fetches message history (30-day window), normalizes to ConnectorDocuments |
| `list_channels()` | Paginated `conversations.list` with configurable channel types |
| `list_messages()` | Paginated `conversations.history` for a channel with optional `oldest` cursor |
| `list_users()` | Paginated `users.list` returning all workspace members |
| `get_user()` | `users.info` for a single user by ID |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: The connector uses **Bot Token authentication** — a single `xoxb-...` token that grants the bot access to the Slack workspace.

children.title: Auth Type
children.content: `oauth2` (Bot Token) — install the Slack App in your workspace, then copy the Bot User OAuth Token from **OAuth & Permissions**.

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Bot Token | `bot_token` | password | Yes | Slack Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-...) |
| Channel Types | `channel_types` | string | No | Comma-separated types: public_channel, private_channel, mpim, im. Default: public_channel |

children.title: Required Bot Token Scopes
children.content:
| Scope | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `channels:read` | List public channels |
| `channels:history` | Read public channel message history |
| `users:read` | List workspace members |
| `groups:read` | List private channels (optional) |
| `groups:history` | Read private channel history (optional) |

children.title: Token Security
children.content: Bot tokens do not expire unless revoked. Shielva stores the token AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest via the vault. The token can be revoked at any time from the Slack App settings under OAuth & Permissions → Revoke Tokens.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content: All configuration is stored in the connector's `config` dict (encrypted at rest by Shielva vault).

children.title: Config Keys
children.content:
| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `bot_token` | Slack Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-...) |
| `channel_types` | Comma-separated channel types to sync (default: public_channel) |

children.title: Channel Types Reference
children.content:
| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `public_channel` | All public channels in the workspace |
| `private_channel` | Private channels the bot is a member of |
| `mpim` | Group direct messages |
| `im` | Direct messages with individual users |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are implemented in `connector.py` and delegate HTTP calls to `SlackHTTPClient` in `client/http_client.py`.

children.title: install()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def install(self) -> InstallResult`

Checks that `bot_token` is present in config. Does not make network calls — token validity is confirmed at `health_check()`.

**Returns:**
- `InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED)` when bot_token is present
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` when bot_token is absent or empty

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def health_check(self) -> HealthCheckResult`

Calls `POST https://slack.com/api/auth.test` to verify the token. Returns the workspace name in the message.

**Returns:**
- `HealthCheckResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to workspace: Acme Corp')`
- `HealthCheckResult(health=DEGRADED, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` on auth errors
- `HealthCheckResult(health=DEGRADED, auth_status=FAILED)` on network errors

children.title: sync()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def sync(self, full=False, since=None, kb_id='') -> SyncResult`

Fetches all channels via `conversations.list`, then for each channel fetches message history via `conversations.history` (default: last 30 days).

**Parameters:**
- `full` — if True and since is None, fetches all history (no `oldest` filter)
- `since` — datetime; only fetch messages newer than this timestamp
- `kb_id` — knowledge base ID for ingestion (SDK mode only)

**Returns:** `SyncResult` with counts of found/synced/failed documents.

Status is `COMPLETED` when no failures occur, `PARTIAL` when some messages fail normalization, `FAILED` when the channel list or auth fails entirely.

children.title: list_channels()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_channels(self, types='public_channel') -> list[dict]`

Paginated `conversations.list`. Follows `response_metadata.next_cursor` until exhausted.

**Returns:** List of Slack channel objects with `id`, `name`, `is_private`, `is_archived`.

children.title: list_messages()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_messages(self, channel_id, oldest=None, limit=100) -> list[dict]`

Paginated `conversations.history` for a specific channel. Follows cursor pagination.

**Parameters:**
- `channel_id` — Slack channel ID (e.g. C0123ABC)
- `oldest` — Unix timestamp string; only fetch messages after this time
- `limit` — number of messages per page (max 200)

**Returns:** List of Slack message objects.

children.title: list_users()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_users(self) -> list[dict]`

Paginated `users.list`. Returns all workspace members including bots.

**Returns:** List of Slack user objects with `id`, `name`, `real_name`, `profile`, `is_bot`.

children.title: get_user()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def get_user(self, user_id: str) -> dict`

Fetches a single user via `users.info`.

**Returns:** Slack user object.

**Raises:** `SlackNotFoundError` if user does not exist.

sections.title: HTTP Client
sections.content: All HTTP calls are encapsulated in `SlackHTTPClient` (`client/http_client.py`) using `aiohttp`. The connector never calls HTTP directly.

children.title: Methods
children.content:
| Method | Slack API Call | Description |
|--------|---------------|-------------|
| `get_auth_test(token)` | `POST /api/auth.test` | Verify token, returns workspace info |
| `get_conversations_list(token, types, cursor, limit)` | `POST /api/conversations.list` | List channels/DMs with cursor pagination |
| `get_conversations_history(token, channel_id, cursor, limit, oldest)` | `POST /api/conversations.history` | Messages in a channel |
| `get_users_list(token, cursor, limit)` | `POST /api/users.list` | List workspace members |
| `get_user_info(token, user_id)` | `POST /api/users.info` | Single user by ID |

children.title: Response Envelope
children.content:
All Slack API responses follow the pattern `{"ok": true/false, ...}`. The `_check_slack_response()` method inspects the `ok` field and maps the `error` string to typed exceptions:

| Slack Error | Exception |
|-------------|----------|
| `invalid_auth`, `not_authed`, `token_revoked`, etc. | `SlackAuthError` |
| `ratelimited` | `SlackRateLimitError` |
| `channel_not_found`, `user_not_found` | `SlackNotFoundError` |
| Connection/timeout | `SlackNetworkError` |
| Other API errors | `SlackError` |

children.title: Retry Logic
children.content:
`with_retry()` in `helpers/utils.py` wraps API calls with exponential back-off:
- `max_attempts=3` by default
- Back-off: 1s, 2s (doubles each attempt)
- Skips retry on `SlackAuthError` — re-installing with a valid token is required
- Retries on `SlackError`, `SlackNetworkError`, `SlackRateLimitError`

children.title: ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "id": "<sha256(channel_id + ts)[:16]>",
  "type": "slack_message",
  "title": "#general — @alice",
  "content": "Channel: #general\nUser: U0123ABC\nTimestamp: 1718784000.000100\nMessage: Hello team!",
  "metadata": {
    "channel_id": "C0123ABC",
    "channel_name": "general",
    "ts": "1718784000.000100",
    "user": "U0123ABC",
    "thread_ts": null,
    "subtype": "",
    "connector_id": "slack_connector",
    "tenant_id": "Tenant-f9184cb7",
    "source": "slack"
  }
}
```

children.title: Document ID Stability
children.content: The `id` field is `SHA-256(channel_id + message_ts)` truncated to 16 hex characters. This is stable across syncs — the same message always produces the same Shielva document ID, enabling upsert deduplication without storing seen-ts lists.

sections.title: Exception Hierarchy
sections.content:
All exceptions inherit from `SlackError` (base) in `exceptions.py`:

```
SlackError (base)
├── SlackAuthError         — invalid_auth, not_authed, token_revoked, missing_scope
├── SlackNetworkError      — connectivity failures, aiohttp.ClientError
├── SlackRateLimitError    — ratelimited
└── SlackNotFoundError     — channel_not_found, user_not_found
```

Callers can catch the base `SlackError` or specific subclasses for finer control.

children.title: Common Errors
children.content:
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `invalid_auth` | Wrong or revoked token | Re-copy the Bot User OAuth Token from Slack App → OAuth & Permissions |
| `missing_scope` | Required permission not added | Add the scope in Slack App → OAuth & Permissions → Bot Token Scopes, then reinstall |
| `channel_not_found` | Bot not invited to channel | Type `/invite @YourApp` in the channel, or use only public_channel type |
| `ratelimited` | Slack API rate limit exceeded | Connector retries automatically with exponential back-off |
| No messages returned | Messages older than 30 days | Adjust sync window using the `since` parameter or set `full=True` |

children.title: Testing
children.content:
Run the full test suite (102 tests, all mocked — no live credentials needed):

```bash
cd /Users/vivekvarshavaishvik/Documents/client_dir/slack_connector
source "/Volumes/V3-SSD/Shielva Project Dirs/.venv/bin/activate"
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

All 102 tests must pass before registering the connector.
